title: 10 best herbal tinctures for neuritis
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for neuritis are liquid extracts made from plants, trees, or shrubs that have been traditionally used to relieve the symptoms of neuritis, a condition characterized by inflammation and damage to nerves. These tinctures help by reducing pain, inflammation, and spasms, allowing for improved nerve function and mobility. Examples include St. John's Wort, Ginkgo biloba, and Turmeric, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can ease symptoms and improve daily life. For example, a person with neuritis in the arm may experience reduced numbness and tingling after taking these tinctures, allowing them to regain their independence and engage in activities they previously avoided.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with neuritis because of its rich antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The tannins present in the maidenhair tree, particularly adenosine and caffeic acid, have been shown to effectively reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system, alleviating symptoms of neuritis such as pain, numbness, and tingling. Additionally, the tincture's flavonoids may help to improve blood flow and nerve conduction, further contributing to its potential benefits in treating this condition.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties help protect the nervous system from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate neuritis symptoms.

Vasodilatory Properties: The vasodilatory effects of Ginkgo biloba tincture help to improve blood flow to the brain and peripheral nerves, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in cases of neuritis.

Neuroprotective Compounds: The neuroprotective compounds present in Ginkgo biloba, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help to prevent or slow down the progression of neurological damage caused by neuritis, reducing pain, numbness, and tingling.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in alkaloids and saponins, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate symptoms of neuritis.

Barks: The bark of the maidenhair tree contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, making it effective for treating inflammation and pain associated with neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves in spring or autumn for optimal potency.
Dry the collected leaves using air circulation or dehydrator on low heat.
Grind 10 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered ginkgo biloba with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar and steep for 2 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and transfer to dark glass bottles storing at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with neuritis because it contains flavonoids and hyperforin, which have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation in the nerves, alleviating symptoms such as numbness, tingling, and pain. Additionally, St John's Wort's sedative effects can promote relaxation and reduce stress, which is often a contributing factor to neuritis. By addressing both the physical and emotional aspects of the condition, herbal tinctures of St John's Wort can provide natural relief from neuritis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: St John's Wort tincture has been shown to reduce inflammation in the nervous system, which helps alleviate symptoms of neuritis such as pain and swelling.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant properties of St John's Wort tincture help protect nerves from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy nerve function and reducing the risk of neuritis.

Pain-relieving and analgesic properties: St John's Wort tincture contains compounds that have been shown to reduce pain perception and inflammation, making it a natural remedy for alleviating symptoms of neuritis such as numbness, tingling, and burning sensations.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the St John's Wort plant contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties beneficial for treating neuritis.

Flowers: The flowers of St John's Wort are rich in hypericin and pseudohypericin, two key active components responsible for the plant's antidepressant and pain-relieving effects, making it effective for treating nerve-related inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried st john's wort flowers at peak potency during the summer solstice.
Combine the dried flowers with 700ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks to allow maceration.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and label accordingly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha tinctures helps with neuritis because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's ability to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the nervous system can help alleviate symptoms such as pain, numbness, and tingling associated with neuritis. Additionally, ashwagandha's adaptogenic properties may help support the body's natural response to stress, which is believed to contribute to the development of neuritis. By targeting the underlying causes of inflammation and oxidative stress, ashwagandha tinctures can provide relief from neuritis symptoms and promote overall nervous system health.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Ashwagandha helps reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate Neuritis symptoms by calming the nervous system and promoting relaxation.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Ashwagandha's antioxidant compounds help neutralize free radicals that cause inflammation in nerve tissues, reducing pain and discomfort associated with Neuritis.

Neuroprotective properties: Ashwagandha has been shown to protect against neuronal damage and promote nerve regeneration, which can help alleviate symptoms such as numbness, tingling, and weakness caused by Neuritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary source of bioactive compounds, such as withanolides and alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to the plant's neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, the underground stems that store energy for the plant, contain a higher concentration of withanolides than roots, making them an effective ingredient in tinctures for neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality ashwagandha roots in the early morning after dew has dried for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested ashwagandha roots by spreading them out in a single layer on paper bags or trays.
Crush 100g of dried ashwagandha roots into smaller pieces using a mortar and pestle to increase surface area.
Steep the crushed ashwagandha roots in 500ml of high-proof vodka for 6 weeks, shaking the mixture daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a dark glass bottle, discarding the solids after use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with neuritis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively alleviate nerve pain and swelling. The curcumin present in turmeric tinctures reduces inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system, providing relief from symptoms such as numbness, tingling, and burning sensations. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help to protect against free radical damage, promoting healthy nerve function and promoting overall recovery.
remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: Turmeric contains a group of compounds called curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system associated with neuritis.

Cicloartisins: The tincture also contains cicloartisins, which have been shown to have analgesic and antispasmodic properties, providing relief from pain and muscle spasms common in conditions like neuritis.

Volatile oils: The volatile oils present in turmeric tinctures have a sedative effect, helping to calm inflammation and reduce nervous activity that contributes to symptoms of neuritis, such as tingling and numbness.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Turmeric rhyzomes contain a high concentration of curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in treating neuritis.

Roots: Turmeric roots have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various nervous system disorders, including neuritis, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried turmeric roots curcuma longa from trusted sources.
Grind the dried turmeric roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar pestle.
Combine one part ground turmeric powder with two parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture at room temperature for six to eight weeks shaking the jar daily allowing the curcuma longa to infuse into the vodka.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids and label the tincture with dosage instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Devil's claw tinctures helps with neuritis because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that target the inflamed nerves, reducing pain and discomfort. The tincture's unique blend of bioactive compounds, such as harpagoside and cimiragenin, has been shown to possess potent analgesic and antispasmodic properties, which help to alleviate the symptoms associated with neuritis, including numbness, tingling, and muscle weakness. By soothing the irritated nerves, devil's claw tinctures provide effective relief from the discomfort of neuritis, promoting overall well-being and quality of life.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Devil's claw contains anti-inflammatory compounds like harpagosides, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the nerves, providing relief from neuritic pain.

Analgesic effects: The tannins and iridoid glycosides present in devil's claw have analgesic properties, helping to numb or reduce the sensation of pain, making it an effective remedy for alleviating neuritis symptoms.

Antioxidant activity: Devil's claw also has antioxidant properties, which help protect nerves from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress, promoting healthy nerve function and reducing inflammation that can contribute to neuritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with neuritis.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes contain high levels of glycosides, which have been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them valuable for treating neuritis-related discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried harpagophytum procumbens roots in the morning when the dew is still present on the plant.
Cut the roots into small pieces and mix them with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil at a ratio of 1:3.
Combine the root mixture with vodka or grain alcohol at a ratio of 1:4 in a clean glass jar, ensuring all ingredients are fully submerged.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking the jar daily during this time.

Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding any solids and labeling the tincture as 1:4 harpagophytum procumbens with neuritis-relieving properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with neuritis because it has a natural ability to calm and soothe the nervous system. The active compounds in valerian root, such as isovaleric acid and valerenic acid, have been shown to reduce inflammation and relax muscle spasms that can contribute to nerve pain and numbness associated with neuritis. Additionally, valerian tinctures can promote a sense of calm and relaxation, which can help alleviate the anxiety and stress that often accompanies neuritis, allowing for a more peaceful and restful recovery.
remedy_properties: Sedative and calming effects: Valerian root contains compounds that calm the nervous system, reducing inflammation and pain associated with neuritis by promoting relaxation and reducing muscle spasms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian's polyphenols and flavonoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which help reduce swelling and discomfort in affected nerves and surrounding tissues.

Nerve protectant and regenerative effects: Valerian contains compounds that support the health of nerve tissue, promoting repair and regeneration of damaged nerves, which can help alleviate symptoms of neuritis such as numbness, tingling, and weakness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Valerian are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have a calming effect on the nervous system and help to reduce inflammation associated with neuritis.
Rhizomes: Valerian rhizomes contain a higher concentration of essential oils than the roots, making them an effective ingredient for tinctures that aim to relieve nerve pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried valeriana officinalis root and leaf material for the desired amount of one pound.
Cleanse the plant material by gently washing it in cold running water and patting dry with a paper towel.
Chop the cleaned plant material into small pieces and pack it tightly into a clean glass jar leaving one inch at the top.
Add 80 proof vodka or grain alcohol to cover the plant material and seal the jar with a lid, allowing it to steep for four weeks in a cool dark place.
After four weeks strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids and storing the tincture in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with neuritis because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which alleviate pressure on the nerves and reduce inflammation. The tincture's active compounds, gingerols and shogaols, also possess analgesic and antispasmodic effects, helping to soothe nerve pain and prevent spasms. Additionally, ginger has antioxidant properties that protect against oxidative stress and damage to nerve tissue, promoting healthy nerve function and alleviating symptoms of neuritis, such as numbness, tingling, and pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's polyphenolic compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation in the nervous system, alleviating symptoms of neuritis.
Pain relief agents: Ginger's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties make it effective in reducing pain associated with neuritis, providing relief from numbness, tingling, and shooting pains.
Antioxidant activity: Ginger's antioxidant compounds help protect the nervous system from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of neuritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, ginger roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate neuralgia and pain associated with neuritis.
Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of ginger contain essential oils and other active ingredients that help reduce inflammation and relieve neuritic symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh ginger root of zingiber officinale in late summer or early fall.
Clean and dry the harvested ginger roots then chop them into small pieces weighing around 250 grams.
Combine the chopped ginger with 750 milliliters of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar and stir well.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for at least six weeks or up to three months shaking daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tinctures helps with neuritis because they contain a unique combination of flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids that work synergistically to soothe and calm inflamed nerves. The anti-inflammatory properties of maypop help reduce swelling and irritation in the nerve tissue, while its analgesic effects ease pain and discomfort. Additionally, maypop's ability to relax muscle spasms can help alleviate symptoms associated with neuritis, such as numbness, tingling, and weakness.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Maypop tincture helps to relax and calm nerve spasms, reducing discomfort and pain associated with neuritis.
Sedative property: The sedative properties of maypop tincture can help to reduce anxiety and stress, which are often exacerbating factors for neuritis symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory property: Maypop tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the affected nerves, providing relief from numbness, tingling, and pain associated with neuritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Maypop plant are rich in bioactive compounds, making them effective in treating neuritis by reducing inflammation and pain.
Barks: The barks of the Maypop plant contain flavonoids and other phytochemicals that help to soothe nerve damage and alleviate symptoms of neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest passiflora incarnata flowers in mid-summer when they are fully bloomed, using scissors or pinch off stems just above leaf nodes.
Dry the fresh flowers in a warm place for 7-10 days, stirring daily to ensure even drying and prevent mold growth.
Combine 500g of dried passiflora incarnata flowers with 1000ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, leaving at least 1 inch headspace above the mixture.
Allow the tincture to macerate for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction and prevent settling, then strain through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container.
Store the finished passiflora incarnata tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid, labeling it with the date, ingredients, and recommended dosage of 20-40 drops, 3 times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with neuritis because of its ability to soothe and calm irritated nerves. The herb contains compounds like rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the affected nerve tissues. Additionally, lemon balm's mild sedative effects can promote relaxation and reduce stress, common contributors to neuritis. By addressing these underlying factors, herbal lemon balm tinctures may provide relief from symptoms such as numbness, tingling, and burning sensations associated with neuritis.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Lemon balm tincture's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the nervous system, which can contribute to neuritis symptoms such as pain and numbness.

Anxiolytic and Sedative Properties: The anxiolytic and sedative compounds present in lemon balm tincture can help to calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that may exacerbate neuritis symptoms and promoting a sense of relaxation and relief.

Pain-Relieving and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Lemon balm's essential oils, such as citral and nerol, have been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with neuritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots contain a higher concentration of essential oils and bioactive compounds, which makes them ideal for extracting the active ingredients needed to alleviate neuritis symptoms.

Leaves: The leaves are rich in rosmarinic acid, a key compound responsible for the plant's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them suitable for treating the inflammation and pain associated with neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried melissa officinalis leaves and stems from your garden or local supplier ensuring a minimum of one pound of material.
Cut the plant material into small pieces and combine with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar covering the mixture by at least an inch.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for eight weeks allowing the plant material to macerate in the liquid.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids and squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the fabric.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles filling them to the top and store in a cool place for up to five years ensuring a consistent potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with neuritis because it has a unique ability to numb the pain and reduce inflammation in the affected nerves. The menthol content in peppermint oil helps to increase blood flow to the affected area, which promotes healing and reduces discomfort. Additionally, peppermint's natural anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and irritation, allowing for improved nerve function and reduced neuritis symptoms. This natural remedy can provide quick and effective relief from the pain and discomfort associated with neuritis.
remedy_properties: Analgesic and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Peppermint tincture helps to relieve pain and inflammation associated with neuritis, by reducing prostaglandins and other inflammatory compounds.

Antispasmodic Effects: Peppermint's antispasmodic properties help to relax muscle spasms and reduce nerve irritation, providing relief from the numbness, tingling, or burning sensations characteristic of neuritis.
Cooling and Sedative Properties: The cooling sensation of peppermint tincture can help to calm and soothe irritated nerves, reducing their sensitivity and promoting relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of peppermint are rich in menthol, which helps to relax muscles and reduce inflammation, making them effective in treating neuritis.
Rhyzomes: Peppermint's rhyzomes contain compounds that help to reduce pain and inflammation in the nerves, making them useful for managing the symptoms of neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality peppermint leaves from organic farms or your own garden at dawn.
Cleanse the fresh peppermint leaves by gently rinsing them in cold running water for 30 seconds.
Chop the cleaned peppermint leaves into small pieces using sharp scissors or a food processor to release oils and flavors.
Combine the chopped peppermint leaves with 80% vodka or high-proof grain alcohol at a ratio of 1:2 (1 part leaves to 2 parts solvent) in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it daily for 10 minutes, before straining and bottling the final tincture.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to neuritis that are treated with herbal tinctures are conditions such as sciatica, lumbago, and rheumatoid arthritis. Herbal remedies like St. John's Wort, Willow Bark, and Capsaicin have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, making them effective in soothing nerve inflammation and reducing discomfort associated with these conditions. These tinctures can be used externally or internally to provide relief from pain and stiffness.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat neuritis with herbal tinctures is to combine Turmeric and Ginger. Turmeric's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce pain and swelling, while Ginger's warming effect increases blood flow and reduces inflammation. Mix equal parts of each tincture in water or oil and take 2-3 times a day. You can also add Valerian root to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety. Always consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new herbal regimen.
